steps.title: Identify the graph's decisive data
steps.content: The passage constrains us to organizations filing Form 990-PF, and the question asks which choice most effectively uses data from the graph. The key is finding a claim about penalty percentages that you can directly verify from the bar heights.

steps.title: Verify the claim against the graph
steps.content:
Choice D asserts that undistributed income penalties were a larger share of the total in 2003 than in 2005.

The graph confirms this directly: undistributed income shows 55% in 2003 vs. 40% in 2005.

Choices A, B, and
  • C fail this test—A introduces Form 990 filers outside the graph's scope
  • B claims averages the percentage chart doesn't provide
  • C misstates the self-dealing trend
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
